findings.title: Mobile touch flow — onTouchStart bypasses lore toast
evidence: onTouchStart (line 1001-1004) calls onPointerAction directly on touchstart.
onPointerAction (line ~960-992) contains only the click/activation path (zoneClickCallback + SFX + door flash logic).
The lore toast is built exclusively inside onMouseMove (lines 919-935): applyHoverTo, zoneToast.textContent, zoneToast.style.opacity='1', ariaLive announcement.
onTouchMove (line 996-999) does delegate to onMouseMove, but touchmove only fires after finger movement — a tap that does not move never triggers it.
Result: tap-without-move activates the zone with zero hover state, zero toast, zero SFX hover sound.
findings.correct_fix: Split touch into two phases: touchstart → call onMouseMove (sets hover + shows toast + shimmer SFX), touchend → call onPointerAction only if touch did not move (touchMoved guard using a stored start position vs. end position delta, e.g. >8px threshold = scroll, skip activation). This mirrors the desktop hover→click sequence on mobile.
findings.false_positive_risk: LOW — code paths are clearly separate; no conditional in onTouchStart that would show the toast.

findings.title: Dead write — crystalData.sphere.position.y after GLB load
evidence: LairGLBAssets.ts line 334: 'if (proceduralGroups?.crystalSphere) proceduralGroups.crystalSphere.visible = false' — the procedural sphere is hidden when crystal_ball.glb loads successfully.
MerlinLairScene.ts line 1134: 'crystalData.sphere.position.y = -1.0 + floatSin * 0.04' runs every frame unconditionally in the update loop.

Once crystalGLBGroup is set (line 831 callback), line 1136 correctly animates the GLB group instead. Line 1134 continues writing to a hidden mesh — it costs one float write per frame with no visible effect.
Comment on line 1131 ('procedural sphere uses -1.0 (always correct); GLB uses crystalGLBBaseY delta') acknowledges the dual path but does not gate line 1134.
This is a dead write, not a correctness bug: the sphere is invisible so the y-update has no rendering impact.
findings.correct_fix: Gate line 1134 with 'if (!crystalGLBGroup)'. This matches the existing pattern on line 1136 ('if (crystalGLBGroup)') and eliminates the dead write. One-line change, zero risk.
findings.false_positive_risk: NONE — visibility=false confirmed at line 334, gate pattern already used one line below at 1136.

findings.title: InstancedMesh double-dispose — c52 playtester claim
evidence: Three.js r170 InstancedMesh.dispose() source: InstancedMesh extends Mesh, and Mesh.dispose() dispatches only a 'dispose' event on the object itself — it does NOT internally call geometry.dispose() or material.dispose().

Three.js design: dispose() on geometry and material are intentionally left to the caller. This is documented behavior and unchanged through r170. The renderer listens to the 'dispose' event on Geometry/Material objects, not on Mesh.

The comment at lines 1209-1212 correctly explains why obj.dispose() is called first (to deallocate the instanceMatrix GPU buffer / InstancedBufferAttribute), and geometry.dispose() + material.dispose() follow for the base geometry and material GPU resources.

These three calls target three distinct GPU resources: (1) instanceMatrix buffer via InstancedMesh.dispose(), (2) vertex attribute buffers via geometry.dispose(), (3) texture/shader program via material.dispose().
There is no double-dispose: each dispose() call targets a different GPU object. The c52 report was incorrect.
findings.correct_fix: No fix needed. The three-call pattern is correct and necessary. The c52 false positive likely arose from misreading the call sequence as redundant without checking Three.js internals.
findings.false_positive_risk: N/A — this IS the false positive.

summary.Q1_mobile_touch_flow: CONFIRM — UX regression, medium severity. touchstart activates without hover/toast. Fix: touchstart→hover, touchend→activate with touchMoved guard.
summary.Q2_dead_write_sphere: CONFIRM — dead write post-GLB-load, low severity (no visible bug). Fix: gate with 'if (!crystalGLBGroup)'.
summary.Q3_instanced_mesh_dispose: FALSE_POSITIVE — Three.js r170 InstancedMesh.dispose() does NOT call geometry/material dispose internally. Three explicit calls are correct and non-redundant.

action_required.action: Refactor onTouchStart to call onMouseMove (hover+toast), move activation to onTouchEnd with touchMoved guard
Add 'if (!crystalGLBGroup)' guard to eliminate dead write on hidden sphere
